stories.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
stories.summary: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

stories.headline: Trump and Xi Enter China Summit Distracted by Iran War
stories.summary: The war in Iran has cast a shadow of uncertainty on both superpowers, dimming early hopes that they could begin to address the larger issues that have frayed their relationship.

stories.headline: Trump skirts question on US ‘red lines’ for Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Trump skirted a question from a reporter about whether he has a ‘red line’ that would end the ceasefire with Iran.

stories.headline: Israel killing at least one child a week in occupied West Bank, UN says
stories.summary: Israeli military and settlers have killed at least one Palestinian child a week on average in the occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

stories.headline: How one Palestinian teen’s life changed forever after Israeli gunfire
stories.summary: Amir Othman was 16 when Israeli soldiers shot him on a hill near Nablus in the occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Lamine Yamal’s Palestine flag wave hailed by fans, activists and athletes
stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza, football fans from around the world and activists laud Barca and Spain footballer.

stories.headline: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.headline: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
stories.summary: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.

stories.headline: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why
stories.summary: There are calls to investigate the 16 March airstrike - which the UN says likely killed more - as a war crime.

stories.headline: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy not implicated in a major graft probe, anti-corruption investigators say
stories.summary: “The President of Ukraine has neither been nor is currently a subject of the investigation,” the head of Ukraine’s anti-corruption bureau said on Tuesday, as the president’s former chief of staff appeared in court for a bail hearing.

stories.headline: Why is Iran increasingly targeting the UAE in its war messaging?
stories.summary: The UAE's close ties to the US and Israel have placed the Gulf country under Iranian suspicion.

stories.headline: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.

stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.headline: Food prices could skyrocket, if no measures taken to tackle fertilizer crisis, farmer unions warn
stories.summary: The fertilizer crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is tripling costs for farmers. “Less fertilizers means less production for wheat. That’s the basis for bread,” warns COPA President, Massimiliano Giansanti.

stories.headline: EU needs to delay social media access for children - von der Leyen
stories.summary: She told an EU summit that an expert panel was due to come up with steps on how to protect minors online by July.

stories.headline: Ex-Philippine leader Duterte's drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest
stories.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, a former national police chief, has sought refuge in the senate.

stories.headline: Starmer clings to power as four ministers resign

stories.headline: UK borrowing costs surge as Starmer leadership crisis rattles bond markets
stories.summary: Thirty-year gilt yield at highest this century as cabinet ministers pressure PM to consider his position

stories.headline: Blue Owl retail fundraising evaporates amid private credit concerns
stories.summary: Fund it operates took in fraction of capital from year-ago level as worries mount over potential surge in loan defaults

stories.headline: US Soy Growers Want China Commitments as Season Slips Away
stories.summary: As US President Donald Trump travels to Beijing for a summit this week with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, American soybean farmers are in a now-familiar spot: Fields are getting planted when demand from their biggest customer is uncertain.

stories.headline: ECB Rate Hikes Increasingly Likely, Nagel Tells Handelsblatt
stories.summary: The probability that the European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs due to the Iran war is rising, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

stories.headline: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
stories.summary: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.

stories.headline: BJP leader back to head India's Assam state for second time in a row
stories.summary: Himanta Biswa Sarma is seen as one of the architects of BJP's rapid growth in India's northeast.

stories.headline: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".

stories.headline: Kalshi Judge Predicts Tribe Will Win Block on Sports Wagers
stories.summary: A tribal nation is likely to succeed in blocking Kalshi from offering sports contracts on its land, a federal judge said in what appears to be the first ruling of its kind against the prediction market operator.

stories.headline: Vietnam Urges US Navy to Let Oil Tanker Through Its Blockade
stories.summary: Vietnam’s state oil company urged the US to let a supertanker pass through an American naval blockade outside of the Persian Gulf, saying the shipment is critical to the Asian country’s economy.

stories.headline: Family Offices Build Stake in Niche Miner After Stock Soars 900%
stories.summary: For decades, the risky world of early-stage mining ventures was the playground of institutional funds and commodity trading houses. But after a more than 900% rally in a little-known Canadian potash developer last year, a new class of investors is moving in: family offices.

stories.headline: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
stories.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

stories.headline: US LNG Pioneer Charif Souki Vows He Will Never Go Public Again
stories.summary: Natural gas entrepreneur Charif Souki’s latest venture will remain closely held after his previous two companies pursued public offerings.

stories.headline: Providence Equity Considers Acquisition of Gamma Communications
stories.summary: Providence Equity Partners is among suitors exploring an acquisition of Gamma Communications Plc, the London-listed telecommunication services company, people with knowledge of the matter said.

stories.headline: Junk-Rated Firms Rush to Reprice Risky Debt as Demand Surges
stories.summary: Junk-rated firms are rushing to reprice and refinance debt, seizing on renewed investor appetite for riskier US dollar-denominated loans to slash borrowing costs.

stories.headline: Trump’s approach on China has changed, now appears focused on getting along
stories.summary: In his first term, the president demanded fundamental shifts in China’s economic model. Now, he has copied parts of how Beijing does business.

stories.headline: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call

stories.headline: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
stories.summary: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

stories.headline: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader's descendants

stories.headline: Advocaat, 78, set for World Cup record after Curacao return
stories.summary: Dick Advocaat makes a surprise return as Curacao's head coach after his successor Fred Rutten resigned just a month out from their World Cup debut.

stories.headline: How social media turned Indian film star Vijay into a political force

stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.

stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.
